name: Neutral posture and alignment
description: Stand with head, shoulders, hips, knees, and ankles stacked vertically along a plumb line with a neutral pelvis.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 3-second hold: horizontal distance between head.(x,z) and root.(x,z) is < 0.06 m; horizontal distance between neck.(x,z) and root.(x,z) is < 0.04 m; the midpoint of left_ankle and right_ankle is within 0.05 m horizontally of root.(x,z); the angle at each knee (vector hip→knee to vector knee→ankle) is between 170° and 180° (extended but not hyperextended); shoulder_mid.y - root.y is between 0.40 m and 0.65 m (depending on user height) and varies less than 0.02 m across the hold.
drill_description: Stand against a wall: heels, glutes, shoulder blades, and back of head lightly touching. Engage core. Hold 15s. Step away from wall and try to maintain alignment for another 15s. Repeat once.
mastery_threshold: Alignment criteria held continuously for 3 seconds on 3 consecutive attempts.
sources: Karen Clippinger, Dance Anatomy and Kinesiology  (Ch. 3, 'The Spine'),  archive.org/details/danceanatomykine2007clip
Dance Magazine, 'How to Actually Find Neutral'
Georgina Butler, citing Clippinger and IADMS Deckert 2009, georginabutler.co.uk/dance-resource-alignment-and-posture
CI Training PDF excerpt, Kerr et al. alignment rating, citraining.com/pdfs/Emerging-Concepts-Posture.pdf

name: Finding the beat and counting in 8s
description: Detect the song's pulse and group it into 8-count phrases, hitting count 1 and 5 as the strong beats.
measurable_success_criterion: While stationary, the user produces a downward head displacement (head.y reaches a local minimum) within ±100 ms of each metronome downbeat (count 1 and count 5) for a 16-count phrase. At least 14 of 16 head-min events are within ±100 ms of the corresponding beat.
drill_description: App plays a 90 BPM metronome with accented count 1 and 5. User bobs head down on every count for 8, then only on 1 and 5 for the next 8, then claps (hands meet in front: wrist-to-wrist distance < 0.10 m) on 2 and 4 of the next 8.
mastery_threshold: 14/16 head-min events within ±100 ms across 3 consecutive 16-count trials.
common_mistakes: Rushing ahead of the beat when uncertain
sources: Classical Ballet and All That Jazz, classicalballetandallthatjazz.com/2022/06/23/dancers-and-musicality-how-we-count/

name: Full weight transfer between feet
description: Shift body center fully from one supporting foot to the other so the unloaded foot can lift freely off the floor.
measurable_success_criterion: Within a 4-count metronome cycle: on count 1, root.x is within 0.05 m of right_ankle.x AND left_ankle.y rises at least 0.05 m above its minimum (the 'lift test'); on count 3, root.x is within 0.05 m of left_ankle.x AND right_ankle.y rises at least 0.05 m. The torso (vector root→neck) tilt from vertical stays under 10° throughout (no body tipping).
drill_description: Stand parallel, hip-width. On count 1 shift fully to right foot, lift left foot 5 cm. Return center on 2. On 3 shift fully to left foot, lift right. Return on 4. Repeat 8 cycles at 80 BPM.
mastery_threshold: Lift criterion met on both sides for 6 of 8 cycles, with torso tilt under 10°, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Tilting torso instead of pushing off the standing leg (Joy of Dance)
sources: Joy of Dance, 'Fundamentals Part One: Weight Transfer', joyofdance.ca/blog-posts/fundamentals-part-one-weight-transfer
Number Analytics, 'Ultimate Guide Weight Transfer Dance Pedagogy', numberanalytics.com/blog/ultimate-guide-weight-transfer-dance-pedagogy

name: Down-bounce (hip-hop down groove)
description: Rhythmic vertical oscillation of the body with the accent on the downbeat: knees flex on count 1, extend between counts.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 16-count phrase at 90–120 BPM: root.y reaches a local minimum within ±80 ms of every downbeat, and a local maximum within ±80 ms of every 'and' (mid-beat). Peak-to-trough amplitude of root.y is between 0.04 m and 0.12 m. Each knee angle decreases by at least 8° between the up-peak and the down-trough.
drill_description: At 90 BPM, soft parallel stance. On every count, sink into knees (root.y down); between counts, extend (root.y up). 16 counts down-bounce, then 16 counts up-bounce (invert phase), then 16 alternating every 4 counts.
mastery_threshold: 14/16 root-min events within ±80 ms of downbeat and amplitude >0.04 m, across 3 consecutive trials.
sources: Buddha Stretch, '4 Main Grooves of Hip-Hop', Howcast, howcast.com/videos/505184
Beyond The Moves, 'Hip-Hop Foundation Moves: The Bounce', beyondthemoves.com/blog/hiphop-dance-foundation-moves-the-bounce-video-1-of-13

name: Up-bounce (hip-hop up groove)
description: Vertical oscillation with the accent inverted: body lifts on the downbeat, sinks between.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 16-count phrase: root.y reaches a local maximum within ±80 ms of every downbeat and a local minimum within ±80 ms of every 'and'. Amplitude 0.04–0.12 m. Each knee angle increases by at least 8° from the down-trough up to the up-peak.
drill_description: Same as down-bounce drill but inverted: start with knees bent, extend up on count 1, sink between counts. 32 counts at 90 BPM.
mastery_threshold: 14/16 root-max events within ±80 ms across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Lifting heels off the floor on the up (becomes a hop)
sources: Buddha Stretch, '4 Main Grooves of Hip-Hop' (Howcast)
Movement Nation 'down bounce vs up bounce', tiktok.com/@movementnation/video/7273045511639190785

name: Demi-plié / soft knees stance
description: A stable, slightly-bent-knee stance used as the launch position for hip isolations and most groove work.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 3-second hold: each knee angle is between 150° and 170°; both ankles within 0.05 m of being directly under their respective hip (|ankle.x − hip.x| < 0.05 m and |ankle.z − hip.z| < 0.06 m); root.y is at least 0.05 m below the user's standing-tall baseline root.y; vertical line through root.x stays within 0.04 m of midpoint of the two ankles.
drill_description: Stand parallel, feet hip-width. Bend knees to a soft demi-plié, hold 5s. Return up, hold 2s. Repeat 8 times.
mastery_threshold: Hold criteria met for 3 seconds on 3 consecutive reps.
common_mistakes: Going too deep into a squat
sources: Bob Rizzo, 'Isolation Inspiration', Dance Spirit ('make sure you're in demi-plié when isolating your hips'),  dancespirit.com/isolation_inspiration-2326058096.html
Clippinger, Dance Anatomy and Kinesiology, Ch. 4 'Pelvic girdle and hip joint'
Matt Mattox / Bob Boross teaching notes, bobboross.com/bob-boross-dance-teaching-6/styled-6

name: Head isolation
description: Move the head left/right/forward/back independently of the shoulders.
measurable_success_criterion: Across an 8-count drill: head.x translates by at least 0.08 m peak-to-peak relative to the shoulder_mid (midpoint of left_shoulder and right_shoulder), while shoulder_mid.x deviates less than 0.02 m from its initial position. Head pitch (computed from the angle between vector (neck→head) and world +Y) never exceeds 25° backward (to avoid cervical hyperextension per Rizzo).
drill_description: Seated or standing in neutral. Slide head left on 1, center on 2, right on 3, center on 4. Then nod chin down on 5, center on 6, tilt right ear toward shoulder on 7, center on 8. Repeat twice.
mastery_threshold: Head translation > 0.08 m AND shoulder_mid drift < 0.02 m on 6 of 8 counts, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Shoulders move with head (failure to isolate)
sources: Bob Boross 'Head Roll Isolation - Matt Mattox', youtube.com/watch?v=ZIe584fM0dI
Bob Rizzo, Dance Spirit (cervical safety cue)

name: Shoulder isolation (up/down, forward/back, rolls)
description: Lift, drop, push, draw, and circle the shoulders independently of the head and ribcage.
measurable_success_criterion: For each shoulder, across 8 counts: vertical (y) range > 0.05 m and sagittal (z) range > 0.04 m when both motions are sampled; throughout, head.y stays within 0.02 m of its initial position, and the vector root→neck deviates less than 5° from vertical. For a shoulder roll: trace of (shoulder.y, shoulder.z) over 1 beat forms a closed loop with circumference > 0.15 m.
drill_description: Neutral stance. Right shoulder up on 1, down on 2; left up on 3, down on 4. Then forward/back: right forward on 5, back on 6; left forward on 7, back on 8. Then 8 counts of full shoulder rolls (4 forward, 4 back).
mastery_threshold: Range thresholds met with head.y stability across 6 of 8 counts, on 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Shrugging to ears (tension) rather than lifting from joint
sources: Bob Boross 'Shoulder Isolation - Matt Mattox', youtube.com/watch?v=QPiYDA68Jy8
Matt Steffanina Neck/Shoulder Circle tutorial, mattsteffanina.com

name: Chest isolation forward/back
description: Translate the ribcage (approximated as shoulder midpoint) forward and back along the sagittal axis while the pelvis stays still.
measurable_success_criterion: Define shoulder_mid = midpoint(left_shoulder, right_shoulder). For an 8-count drill: shoulder_mid.z − root.z reaches at least +0.05 m forward of its rest value on counts 1,3,5,7 and at least −0.03 m on counts 2,4,6,8. Throughout, root.(x,z) drifts less than 0.03 m from its initial position, and the line between the two shoulders stays within 5° of horizontal (shoulders square — Rizzo).
drill_description: Hands on hips, soft knees. Push chest forward on 1, pull back on 2. 8 reps. Then double-time: forward on every count for 8.
mastery_threshold: Forward displacement > 0.05 m AND root.z drift < 0.03 m on 6 of 8 counts, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Bending at the waist (root moves with chest)
Shoulders shrug forward instead of ribcage translating
sources: Matt Mattox/Bob Boross isolation videos (YouTube, 'Rib Isolation - JAZZ ISOLATION Course', youtube.com/watch?v=43GFq2rho-U)
Bob Rizzo (Dance Spirit) — 'keep shoulders square when isolating the rib cage'
Clippinger, Dance Anatomy and Kinesiology, Ch. 3 (thoracic articulation)

name: Chest isolation side-to-side
description: Translate the ribcage laterally (left and right) while the pelvis stays centered.
measurable_success_criterion: For an 8-count drill: shoulder_mid.x − root.x reaches at least +0.06 m to the right on counts 1,5 and −0.06 m to the left on counts 3,7. root.(x,z) drifts less than 0.03 m. Line between shoulders stays within 8° of horizontal (no tilt — translation only).
drill_description: Hands on hips. Slide ribs right on 1, center on 2, left on 3, center on 4. 4 sets. Then combine: front, right, back, left (box pattern).
mastery_threshold: Lateral displacement > 0.06 m AND root drift < 0.03 m AND shoulder-tilt < 8°, on 6 of 8 counts, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Tilting shoulders instead of translating ribcage
sources: Matt Mattox/Bob Boross rib isolation videos

name: Hip isolation side-to-side
description: Translate the pelvis laterally while the upper body stays still.
measurable_success_criterion: In demi-plié (both knees 150–170°). For 8 counts: root.x reaches at least +0.06 m on counts 1,5 and −0.06 m on counts 3,7, while shoulder_mid.x drifts less than 0.025 m. Both knees stay bent throughout (each knee angle < 175° at all times).
drill_description: Demi-plié, hands on hips. Slide hips right on 1, center on 2, left on 3, center on 4. 4 sets at 90 BPM.
mastery_threshold: Hip displacement > 0.06 m AND shoulder drift < 0.025 m on 6 of 8 counts, across 3 consecutive trials.
sources: Bob Rizzo (Dance Spirit) — 'in demi-plié when isolating hips'
Grokipedia / Mattox — pelvic articulation paired with arms
Clippinger Ch. 4 'Pelvic girdle and hip joint'

name: Hip isolation forward/back (tuck and release)
description: Tilt the pelvis anteriorly (tuck) and posteriorly (release) while the chest stays still.
measurable_success_criterion: In demi-plié. For 8 counts: vector (root → midpoint(left_hip, right_hip)) translates in z by at least 0.04 m forward on counts 1,3,5,7 and 0.03 m back on counts 2,4,6,8. shoulder_mid.z drifts less than 0.025 m relative to its starting value.
drill_description: Demi-plié, hands on hips. Tuck pelvis forward on 1, neutral on 2, sway back on 3, neutral on 4. 4 sets.
mastery_threshold: Pelvic sagittal range > 0.04 m AND shoulder_mid stability on 6 of 8 counts, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Bending knees more on the tuck (vertical drop instead of tilt)
Too much lumbar extension on the release
sources: STEEZY David Lee body roll  tutorial cues, youtube.com/watch?v=CSD0dhibOzY

name: Hip pop / sharp accent
description: A sharp, accented lateral hip displacement followed by an immediate stop.
measurable_success_criterion: Within 120 ms of the target beat, root.x velocity exceeds 0.6 m/s in one direction, then within the next 120 ms drops to below 0.1 m/s and root.x stays within 0.02 m of its arrival position for at least 200 ms after. shoulder_mid.x drifts less than 0.03 m during the same window.
drill_description: Demi-plié, hands on hips, at 100 BPM. Sharp hip pop to right on count 1 (hold), reset on 2; pop to left on 3 (hold), reset on 4. 8 cycles.
mastery_threshold: Velocity-and-stop criterion met on 6 of 8 pops, across 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: No real acceleration — slow slide masquerading as pop

name: Knee bend articulation
description: Independently flex and extend each knee on beat without losing pelvic stability.
measurable_success_criterion: Across 8 counts at 90 BPM: each knee's angle changes by at least 20° within a beat (max-min); root.y oscillates with each knee flex (peak-to-trough > 0.03 m). When one knee flexes by 20°, the other stays within 5° of its starting angle for the unilateral drill, or both flex symmetrically (within 5° of each other) for the bilateral drill.
drill_description: Stand parallel. Right knee bends on 1, extends on 2 (left stays straight). Left knee bends on 3, extends on 4. Then both knees together for 8 counts.
mastery_threshold: Range and symmetry criteria met on 6 of 8 counts, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Hip-hike instead of true knee bend
sources: Mr. Wiggles Hip Hop Camp syllabus, wigzee.biz/contact/book%20hip%20hop%20camp/

name: Ankle pivot
description: Rotate the foot on the ball or heel while the supporting leg axis stays mostly fixed.
measurable_success_criterion: When pivoting on the right foot: right_ankle.(x,z) translates less than 0.04 m while the vector (right_knee → right_hip) rotates by at least 30° around the world Y-axis within one beat. Left_ankle.y rises at least 0.04 m off the floor during the pivot.
drill_description: Stand on right foot, ball-pivot 90° clockwise on count 1, reset on 2. Same counter-clockwise on 3,4. Switch feet. 16 counts total.
mastery_threshold: 30° hip-vector rotation with < 0.04 m ankle translation on 6 of 8 pivots, 3 consecutive trials.
sources: Mr. Wiggles Hip Hop Camp, glides and pivots drills

name: Two-step (basic side step)
description: Step out to one side, bring the other foot to meet it, transferring weight on each step.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 4-count cycle at 90 BPM: on count 1 right_ankle.x increases by at least 0.20 m (step out) and root.x moves at least 0.10 m right; on count 2 left_ankle.x increases by at least 0.18 m (close) and ends within 0.10 m of right_ankle.x. Mirror on counts 3,4 to the left. Both ankles must each lift > 0.03 m above floor minimum during their respective step.
drill_description: At 90 BPM, two-step right-close, left-close, for 16 counts. Then add bounce_down underneath for another 16.
mastery_threshold: Step-out distance + close distance criteria met for 6 of 8 cycles, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Touching instead of stepping (no weight transfer — becomes step-touch)
Steps too wide (looks like a lunge)
Bouncing on the step instead of between
sources: NJHH Squad, notjusthiphop.art/post/mastering-the-basics-of-hip-hop-dance-techniques (defines two-step as weight transfer)

name: Two-step with bounce
description: Two-step layered over a continuous down-bounce groove — the canonical hip-hop social step.
measurable_success_criterion: All two_step criteria hold. Additionally, root.y reaches a local min within ±80 ms of every count (1–4), with amplitude > 0.04 m. Each knee flexes by at least 10° on its corresponding downbeat.
drill_description: At 95 BPM, 16 counts of two-step with continuous down-bounce.
mastery_threshold: Two-step criteria AND bounce criteria met for 6 of 8 cycles, 3 consecutive trials.

name: Step-touch
description: Step to one side then touch the other foot beside it without transferring weight.
measurable_success_criterion: On count 1: right_ankle.x increases by > 0.18 m and root.x by > 0.08 m. On count 2: left_ankle moves to within 0.05 m of right_ankle but left_ankle.y stays within 0.02 m of floor (touch, no lift) AND root.x stays on the right side. Mirror on 3,4.
drill_description: 16 counts of step-touch at 90 BPM, alternating right and left.
mastery_threshold: Touch (no lift) confirmed on 6 of 8 cycles, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Touching too far from the planted foot

name: Forward/back walking patterns
description: Sequential weight transfers traveling forward or backward in time with music.
measurable_success_criterion: For 4 forward steps at 90 BPM: on each count, the leading ankle.z moves forward by > 0.20 m relative to its prior position; root.z advances by at least 0.10 m per count. Torso vector (root → neck) tilt from vertical stays under 12°.
drill_description: Walk forward 4 counts, walk backward 4 counts, repeat for 32 counts at 90 BPM.
mastery_threshold: Step length and root advance met for 6 of 8 counts, 3 consecutive trials.
sources: Number Analytics weight transfer pedagogy guide

name: Side glide / slide
description: Travel laterally without lifting the sliding foot — illusion of frictionless slide.
measurable_success_criterion: Across 4 counts: right_ankle.x increases by at least 0.30 m total, with right_ankle.y staying below 0.03 m above floor throughout (no lift). On each beat, weight is on left foot (root.x within 0.08 m of left_ankle.x) while right slides; or weight transfers cleanly. Knee angle of supporting leg stays between 150° and 170°.
drill_description: Side glide drill: shift weight to left foot, slide right foot out 4 counts then shift weight to right; slide left in 4 counts to meet. 32 counts.
mastery_threshold: Sliding-foot Y < 0.03 m above floor for entire 4-count slide, on 6 of 8 trials.
sources: Mr. Wiggles Hip Hop Camp Day 2 (Floats, Slides & Glides syllabus)

name: Pivot turn (quarter, half, full)
description: Rotate the body around a vertical axis on a supporting foot.
measurable_success_criterion: For a half-turn (180°) on the right foot in 2 counts: vector (left_shoulder → right_shoulder) rotation around world Y reaches 180° ± 20°; right_ankle.(x,z) translates < 0.05 m; root.(x,z) stays within 0.08 m of right_ankle throughout. Spine (root → neck) stays within 10° of vertical.
drill_description: Quarter pivot on right foot count 1, reset count 2. Then half turn. Then full turn (chaîné: step right pivot 180°, step left pivot 180°). 32 counts.
mastery_threshold: Rotation angle and stability met for 6 of 8 turns, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Weight not fully on pivot foot
sources: Joy of Dance — weight transfer and turn failures

name: Cross-step / grapevine
description: Lateral travel with one foot crossing over (or behind) the other.
measurable_success_criterion: 4-count cycle to the right at 90 BPM. Count 1: right_ankle.x − left_ankle.x > 0.20 m (step right). Count 2: left_ankle.x crosses to within 0.05 m of right_ankle.x AND left_ankle.z is at least 0.06 m behind (or 0.06 m forward of) right_ankle.z — i.e., left foot crossed behind (or in front), measured in z. Count 3: right steps right again. Count 4: left touches beside right.
drill_description: Grapevine right 4 counts, grapevine left 4 counts. 32 counts at 90 BPM.
mastery_threshold: Cross-z offset met on 6 of 8 cycles, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Crossing in front when behind is called (or vice versa)
Failing to commit weight on each step

name: Sharp arm extension (point)
description: Fully extend one arm to a target angle and dime-stop on the beat — the foundation of locking 'point' and K-pop point choreography.
measurable_success_criterion: On the target beat: vector (shoulder → elbow → wrist) is colinear within 15° (i.e., elbow angle > 165°); the wrist arrives at its target position with velocity < 0.15 m/s within 100 ms and stays within 0.03 m for the next 250 ms (dime stop). Arm length from shoulder to wrist reaches > 85% of the user's measured arm length.
drill_description: From hands at chest, extend right arm forward sharp on count 1, hold to 2. Extend right side on 3, hold to 4. Up on 5, side on 7. Switch arms.
mastery_threshold: Elbow angle > 165° AND wrist dime-stop achieved on 6 of 8 extensions, 3 consecutive trials.

name: Dime stop (full-body sharp freeze)
description: Bring all tracked joints to a clean velocity-zero arrival on a target beat with no overshoot or oscillation.
measurable_success_criterion: On the target beat (±60 ms): for every joint, velocity magnitude drops below 0.10 m/s within 120 ms of arrival and stays below 0.10 m/s for the next 300 ms; per-joint position stays within 0.025 m of arrival position during that 300 ms window.
drill_description: At 100 BPM, jump into a star pose on count 1 (full dime stop), reset on 2. Pose 2 on 3, reset on 4. 4 distinct poses across 16 counts.
mastery_threshold: Dime stop criterion met on all 17 joints on 5 of 8 attempts, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Joints continue to drift after arrival (ringing)
Some joints stop, others don't (incomplete freeze)
sources: Kid Boogie / Boogie Frantick STEEZY popping curriculum

name: Arm wave
description: Pass a visual wave from one wrist through the elbow, shoulder, across to the other shoulder, elbow, and wrist in sequence.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 4-count right-to-left wave at 100 BPM: the y-coordinate local maximum sequence is right_wrist (count 1), right_elbow (count 1.5), right_shoulder (count 2), left_shoulder (count 2.5), left_elbow (count 3), left_wrist (count 3.5). Each peak must occur within ±100 ms of the prior peak and be at least 0.05 m above the joint's pre-wave baseline.
drill_description: Stand in 'T' (arms out to sides). Wave right to left 4 slow counts each, then 4 left to right, then double-time.
mastery_threshold: Sequential peaks in correct order with timing offsets met on 4 of 6 waves, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Skipping joints (e.g. shoulder pops before elbow)
Joints don't return to baseline between peaks
sources: Matt Steffanina arm wave tutorial, youtube.com/watch?v=K2FFULkmC-g

name: Body roll (forward)
description: Sequential sagittal wave through the torso: chest forward → midsection contracts → pelvis forward.
measurable_success_criterion: Over a 2-beat (1.2s at 100 BPM) window: shoulder_mid.z forward-displacement peaks at time t1; (shoulder_mid.z − root.z) reaches its minimum (chest pulled back, abs tucked) at t2 with t2 − t1 in [0.20s, 0.50s]; root.z forward-displacement peaks at t3 with t3 − t2 in [0.20s, 0.50s]. Each segment's z-displacement from baseline > 0.05 m. Knee angles stay between 150° and 175° throughout.
drill_description: Demi-plié. Slow body roll over 4 counts (chest count 1–2, contract 3, hips 4). 4 reps. Then 2-count rolls for 8 reps.
mastery_threshold: Sequential timing met for 4 of 6 rolls, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Chest and hips move simultaneously (no wave)
Bending forward at waist instead of translating
sources: STEEZY David Lee body roll, youtube.com/watch?v=CSD0dhibOzY; steezy.co/posts/how-to-do-an-arched-back-body-roll

name: Body roll (reverse)
description: Bottom-up sagittal wave: pelvis forward → abs out → chest contracts back.
measurable_success_criterion: Sequence inverted: root.z peaks at t1; (shoulder_mid.z − root.z) min at t2 with t2 − t1 in [0.20s, 0.50s]; shoulder_mid.z peaks back at t3 with t3 − t2 in [0.20s, 0.50s]. Each segment displacement > 0.05 m.
drill_description: Demi-plié. Reverse roll over 4 counts. 4 reps slow, then 8 reps at 2-count tempo.
mastery_threshold: Reversed sequential timing met for 4 of 6 rolls, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Defaulting back to forward roll order

name: Body wave (vertical)
description: Sagittal undulation timed so the wave appears to travel down (or up) the body fluidly across 2 counts.
measurable_success_criterion: For a top-down body wave: head.z peak at t1, shoulder_mid.z peak at t2, root.z peak at t3, knee_mid.z (midpoint of left_knee and right_knee) peak at t4, with each Δt in [0.10s, 0.35s] and the four peaks within the 2-beat window ±100 ms total. Each segment's z-displacement > 0.04 m. Reverse for bottom-up wave.
drill_description: 4 slow top-down waves (4 counts each), then 4 bottom-up. Finish with 4 alternating.
mastery_threshold: Sequential peaks within timing windows on 4 of 6 waves, 3 consecutive trials.

name: Running man
description: Alternating knee-lift and back-slide of the opposite foot with contralateral arm swing — the canonical bounce groove.
measurable_success_criterion: For a 2-count cycle at 100 BPM: on count 1 the right knee lifts (right_knee.y rises > 0.10 m above its baseline) while left_ankle.z slides backward by > 0.15 m relative to root.z; on count 2 roles swap (left_knee.y > +0.10 m, right_ankle.z back > 0.15 m). Arm swing: left_wrist forward (z > root.z + 0.10 m) when right knee is up (contralateral pattern). Repeat for 8 cycles.
drill_description: 32 counts of running man at 100 BPM; first 8 without arms, last 24 with contralateral arm swing.
mastery_threshold: Knee lift + slide + contralateral arm criteria met on 6 of 8 cycles, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Same-side arm and leg (ipsilateral instead of contralateral)
No actual slide of the back foot
Pelvis bobs instead of full bounce
sources: Buddha Stretch / Henry Link 'Old School Hip Hop Dictionary', pancocojams.blogspot.com/2020/02/old-school-hip-hop-dance-video.html

name: The Smurf
description: Continuous down-groove with alternating cross-body arm swings.
measurable_success_criterion: 16-count drill at 100 BPM. root.y bounces with peak-to-trough > 0.04 m on every count (down accent — root.y min on the count). On counts 1,3,5,7 the right wrist crosses to the left of left_shoulder.x; on counts 2,4,6,8 the left wrist crosses to the right of right_shoulder.x. Wrist arc traces > 0.30 m horizontal path per swing.
drill_description: Bounce in place 8 counts; add cross-body arm swings (Howcast 'swimming side to side') for 16 counts.
mastery_threshold: Bounce + arm-crossing criteria met for 12 of 16 counts, 3 consecutive trials.

name: Side and front-back rock
description: Pendulum-like weight shift between two grounded points; foundational to top-rock and many TikTok intros.
measurable_success_criterion: Side rock: across 8 counts at 90 BPM, root.x oscillates with amplitude > 0.08 m per swing; on each count one ankle bears > 80% of weight (root.x within 0.06 m of that ankle.x) while the other ankle.y > 0.03 m off floor or lightly tapping. Front-back rock: root.z oscillates with amplitude > 0.08 m per swing.
drill_description: 8 counts side rock, 8 counts front-back rock, then 16 counts side rock with crossed-arm swing.
mastery_threshold: Root oscillation amplitude and weight-bearing met for 6 of 8 counts, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Tap with no real weight commit
Bouncing on every count instead of rocking
sources: Buddha Stretch interview (Soul One NY) — 'precursor to hip-hop was rocking'
Dansehallerne workshop syllabus (Anastajia Olescuka) — 'groove, bounce and rock' as 3 foundations

name: The Lock (locking freeze)
description: Sudden arrival of the arms into a held position with no overshoot — Don Campbell's canonical lock.
measurable_success_criterion: On the target beat: both elbow angles drop into a target range of 60°–90° within 120 ms (the locked-elbow shape); both wrists arrive with velocity < 0.10 m/s and stay within 0.025 m of arrival for at least 400 ms; both shoulders within 5° of horizontal alignment.
drill_description: Stop N' Go drill: step side-to-side, on every other count freeze into the lock pose. 32 counts.
mastery_threshold: Lock criteria met on 5 of 8 locks, 3 consecutive trials.
common_mistakes: Soft, gradual arrival instead of sharp
Body keeps bouncing while arms lock

name: Woah (TikTok signature elbow drop)
description: Pull a fist sharply down to chest level while the opposite hand traces a small circle, ending in a held freeze.
measurable_success_criterion: Within a 1-beat window: right_wrist.y drops by at least 0.20 m, ending with right_elbow angle in 75°–110° and right_wrist within 0.10 m of shoulder_mid in (x,y); left_wrist traces a path of length > 0.20 m around chest level (left_wrist.y within 0.10 m of shoulder_mid.y) within 400 ms; both wrists then dime-stop (velocity < 0.10 m/s for 300 ms).
drill_description: Practice 8 individual woahs at 80 BPM, alternating sides each rep. Then 4 at 100 BPM.
mastery_threshold: Drop depth + dime-stop met on 6 of 8 attempts, 3 consecutive trials.

name: 8-count: Hit-Roll-Step
description: Hip-hop fundamental 8-count: chest hit, body roll up, step-out with arm sweep, recover to point.
measurable_success_criterion: Beats 1–2: chest hit (shoulder_mid.z forward > 0.05 m on beat 1, return on beat 2) with root.y in down-bounce. Beats 3–4: body_roll_forward criteria met across these 2 beats. Beats 5–6: right_ankle.x − left_ankle.x > 0.20 m by beat 5 (step out) AND right_wrist traces > 0.30 m horizontal sweep. Beats 7–8: feet return within 0.10 m of each other AND right arm dime-stops in a point (elbow > 165° on beat 8).
drill_description: Walk through the 8 at half-tempo (80 BPM) twice, then full-tempo (100 BPM) twice. Final pass: full-tempo with mirror.
mastery_threshold: All four phrase criteria met on 3 consecutive 8-counts.
sources: Phil Wright / CLI Studios Hip-Hop Fundamentals, clistudios.com/dance-blog/dance-basics/hip-hop-fundamentals-beginners-need-to-know/

name: 8-count: Woah-Wave-Step
description: TikTok signature 8-count: two-step setup, body wave, woah hit, two-step recovery with arm cross.
measurable_success_criterion: Beats 1–2: two_step_bounce criteria for one cycle. Beats 3–4: body_wave_vertical criteria across 2 beats. Beat 5: woah criteria within ±100 ms of beat 5. Beats 6–8: feet return through two-step pattern with crossed wrists (right_wrist.x < left_shoulder.x AND left_wrist.x > right_shoulder.x) on beat 8.
drill_description: Half-tempo run-through twice, full-tempo twice.
mastery_threshold: All four phrase criteria met on 3 consecutive 8-counts.
common_mistakes: Wave too slow — runs over woah
Forgetting the arm cross on the recovery

name: 8-count: K-pop point hook
description: K-pop chorus-hook 8-count: walk-in, sharp arm point, dime-stop pose, hip roll, arm-heart gesture.
measurable_success_criterion: Beats 1–2: walk_travel forward 2 steps (root.z advances > 0.20 m total). Beat 3: arm_extension criteria (one arm). Beat 4: dime_stop criteria (all joints frozen for 300 ms). Beats 5–6: hip roll — root.x oscillates > 0.06 m AND (root → mid-hip) z-tilt range > 0.04 m. Beats 7–8: both wrists rise above head.y by 0.05 m and come within 0.15 m of each other (arm heart approximated by overhead wrist meet).
drill_description: Slow walkthrough at 80 BPM, then 110 BPM (typical K-pop chorus tempo).
mastery_threshold: All five phrase criteria met on 3 consecutive 8-counts.
common_mistakes: Soft arm extension on the point
sources: STEEZY 'How to Learn K-Pop Dances', steezy.co/posts/how-to-learn-k-pop-dances

name: 8-count: Afrobeats groove block
description: Afrobeats-style 8: gwara-gwara each side, shaku-style crossed-arm step, hip-roll reset.
measurable_success_criterion: Beats 1–2: right gwara — right_knee.y > +0.15 m above baseline while right_ankle pivots (vector left_hip → left_knee rotates around Y by > 20°) AND right elbow traces a circle of arc length > 0.30 m. Beats 3–4: left gwara, mirrored. Beats 5–6: two-step with wrists crossed (right_wrist.x < neck.x AND left_wrist.x > neck.x) for both counts. Beats 7–8: hip roll — root.x oscillates with amplitude > 0.06 m.
drill_description: Half-tempo walkthrough (80 BPM) twice; full-tempo (105 BPM) twice.
mastery_threshold: All four phrase criteria met on 3 consecutive 8-counts.
common_mistakes: Forgetting the arm cross during the shaku step
sources: Izzy Odigie Gwara Gwara tutorial, youtube.com/watch?v=exLc0HjDhzY
Rolling Stone Africa 'African Dance Moves That Travelled the World', rollingstoneafrica.com/culture/african-dance-moves
Native Mag '2010s Africa dance moves guide'

name: 8-count: Marching formation hits
description: K-pop / commercial group-friendly 8: forward marching steps with synchronized arm hits and a closing pose.
measurable_success_criterion: Beats 1–4: 4 forward steps (each beat, leading ankle.z advances > 0.18 m and the trailing knee.y rises > 0.10 m above the standing knee — marching style). Beats 5–6: both wrists punch outward — right_wrist.x − right_shoulder.x > 0.30 m AND left_wrist.x − left_shoulder.x < −0.30 m at peak. Beats 7–8: dime_stop pose with both arms framed at shoulder height (elbow angle > 150° on both sides).
drill_description: Half-tempo twice, then full-tempo (110 BPM) twice; finish with a mirror pass.
mastery_threshold: All three phrase criteria met on 3 consecutive 8-counts.
common_mistakes: Steps too short — no real march

name: Routine: Golden (HUNTR/X)
description: Chorus choreography for 'Golden' by HUNTR/X from KPop Demon Hunters — glide, arrow-shot arm, hip pop, cross-step.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the 16-second chorus loop: DTW score against reference joint-angle vectors ≥ 75%, with skill-weighted sub-scores meeting their per-skill thresholds.
drill_description: Three full chorus passes. Pass 1 at 80% tempo for learning; Pass 2 at 100% tempo with mirrored guide; Pass 3 at 100% tempo unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Arrow-shot arm not fully extended (no dime stop)
sources: Ana Bensig step-by-step tutorial on YouTube

name: Routine: RUDE! (Hearts2Hearts)
description: Chorus of 'RUDE!' by Hearts2Hearts — shoulder-brush, hip sway, large-arm letter shapes spelling 'R-U-D-E'.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~14-second chorus loop: DTW score ≥ 75%, with per-skill sub-scores meeting thresholds. Letter shapes scored as whole-arm geometry (elbow + shoulder angles within ±20° of reference) — finger details ignored.
drill_description: Three full chorus passes. Pass 1 at 80% tempo; Pass 2 at 100% with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Shoulder-brush too small (no shoulder iso engagement)

name: Routine: NOT CUTE ANYMORE (ILLIT)
description: Chorus of ILLIT's 'NOT CUTE ANYMORE' — bratty lower-body stomp, jump on the hook, arm-to-face shush gesture, hip pop attitude pose.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~15-second chorus loop: DTW score ≥ 75%, with per-skill sub-scores meeting thresholds. Jump scored as both ankles.y > 0.12 m above floor with simultaneous lift within 80 ms.
drill_description: Three full chorus passes. Pass 1 at 80%; Pass 2 at 100% with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Shush gesture too small (wrist must reach mouth area = within 0.15 m of head.y)

name: Routine: Gabriela (KATSEYE)
description: Chorus of KATSEYE's 'Gabriela' — driving-wheel two-handed turn, marching-formation steps, big arm sweeps.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~15-second chorus loop: DTW score ≥ 75%, with per-skill sub-scores. Driving-wheel scored as both wrists tracking a virtual ring (each wrist tracing > 0.25 m arc with both within 0.30 m of each other) synchronized on beats.
drill_description: Three full chorus passes. Pass 1 at 80%; Pass 2 with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Hands too far apart on the wheel
Arm sweep doesn't reach full extension on the hook

name: Routine: The Dead Dance (Lady Gaga)
description: Viral 20-second section of Lady Gaga's 'The Dead Dance' from Wednesday S2 — disco-goth arm waves, shoulder isolations, hip sways, signature puppet walk.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~20-second viral section: DTW score ≥ 75%. Puppet walk scored as alternating high-knee + low-knee on consecutive beats (one knee.y > +0.10 m above baseline, the other < +0.03 m).
drill_description: Three full passes. Pass 1 at 80% tempo; Pass 2 with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Shoulder iso too small (Lady Gaga's are exaggerated)
Puppet walk same height each step
Hip sway not synced to beat
sources: Lady Gaga official TikTok (683K likes recreation)
Wednesday S2 Pt2 / Mayhem Ball Tour press coverage

name: Routine: Gnarly (KATSEYE)
description: Iconic dance-break section of KATSEYE's 'Gnarly' — shoulder pops, hip rolls, sassy marching-leg groove.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~14-second break: DTW score ≥ 75%. Shoulder pops scored as alternating shoulder hits — each shoulder.y must spike > 0.04 m within 100 ms of its target beat, dime-stop within 200 ms.
drill_description: Three passes. Pass 1 at 80%; Pass 2 with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Hip rolls become hip sways (lose isolation)
sources: Choreographers: Sohey Sugihara, Grant Gilmore, Sienna Lalau

name: Routine: Soda Pop (Saja Boys)
description: Chorus of Saja Boys' 'Soda Pop' from KPop Demon Hunters — three iconic beats, shoulder pops, drink-sipping hand-to-mouth, sharp arm gestures, leg cross-steps.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~12-second chorus: DTW score ≥ 75%. Drink-sipping scored as right_wrist.y reaches within 0.15 m of head.y while elbow angle drops below 60° on the target beat — finger detail ignored.
drill_description: Three passes. Pass 1 at 80%; Pass 2 with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Sip gesture wrist too far from face
sources: Korea Herald: 970,000+ TikTok content pieces in Korea
Sandra Bensig 'Soda Pop Mirrored' tutorial, YouTube

name: Routine: How It's Done (HUNTR/X)
description: High-energy intro choreography of HUNTR/X's 'How It's Done' — sword-slash arm strikes, formation kicks, sharp turns.
measurable_success_criterion: Across the ~18-second section: DTW score ≥ 75%. Sword-slash scored as wrist trajectory length > 0.50 m within 1 beat ending in dime_stop with arm extension (elbow > 160°). Kick scored as ankle.y > 0.30 m above floor with knee angle > 150°.
drill_description: Three full passes. Pass 1 at 70% tempo (high-energy section needs slow learning); Pass 2 at 100% with mirror; Pass 3 unassisted.
mastery_threshold: DTW score ≥ 80% across 3 consecutive full passes at 100% tempo.
common_mistakes: Strikes too soft — no full arm extension
sources: KPDH soundtrack press; #7 Billboard Global 200 at peak
